Personal information may include:
Even if you already submitted VA Form 21-4142a, we may still need the separate VA Form 21-4142 in order to request your personal information.
Use VA Form 21-4142 to give us permission to request your personal information.

Your claim letter includes the provider address that we need.

AO - Blue Water Notice.friendlyName: Service information related to herbicide exposure
AO - Blue Water Notice.shortDescription: We need information about your service in Vietnam to confirm your exposure to herbicides (like Agent Orange).

blocks.content: If you served on a U.S. Navy or Coast Guard ship off the coast (“blue waters”) of Vietnam between January 9, 1962, and May 7, 1975, we may presume you were exposed to herbicides (like Agent Orange). To confirm your exposure, we need evidence that you spent time in Vietnam or on its inland waterways. Please provide:

items: The approximate dates your ship entered inland waterways, docked, or sent you ashore
Whether you went ashore if your ship docked
If you went ashore from a ship at anchorage, a description of the circumstances

blocks.content: We may not presume Agent Orange exposure if your ship only anchored temporarily in an open deep-water harbor (such as Da Nang, Cam Ranh Bay, or Vung Tau) and you stayed on the ship.

Some Veterans served on smaller, shallow-draft vessels on Vietnam's rivers, canals, estuaries, and delta areas (“brown waters”). If you're one of these Veterans, we may presume you were exposed to herbicides. Tell us the name of your vessel and your dates of service in Vietnam.

content: Use Statement in Support of Claim (VA Form 21-4138) to answer the questions listed in this request under
After completing and signing the form, you can upload or mail it.

blocks.content: Make sure your name and Social Security number are included on any document you upload or send.

AO - Explain how exposed to herbicides.shortDescription: To process your disability claim for herbicide exposure, we need more information from you.
supportAliases: AO - Explain how exposed to herbicides

blocks.content: We need to know how your military duties exposed you to herbicides (like Agent Orange). You may have been exposed if:

items: You served in the Republic of Vietnam between January 9, 1962, and May 7, 1975 (including service in offshore waters or other locations with duty in the Republic of Vietnam)
Your job exposed you to herbicides outside of Vietnam

content: If you served in Vietnam, upload or send us evidence of your service, such as your DD214.

content.text: Learn how to request your military service records from the National Archives
content.testId: Learn how to request your military service records from the National Archives

blocks.content: If you didn’t serve in Vietnam, tell us when, where, and how you were exposed. You can include statements from people who know about your exposure. Any person making a statement should include:

content: Use Statement in Support of Claim (VA Form 21-4138) to answer the questions listed in this request under
After completing and signing the form, you can upload or mail it.

blocks.content: Make sure your name and Social Security number are included on any document you upload or send.

AO - med evid of disab fm herbicide need.shortDescription: To process your disability claim, we need medical information about your exposure to herbicides.
supportAliases: AO - med evid of disab fm herbicide needed

blocks.content: We need medical evidence to process your disability claim for herbicide exposure (like Agent Orange). This evidence should show the diagnosis and earliest symptoms for each disability related to your exposure. Check your claim letter for the specific disabilities we're asking about.

content.content: If you’re claiming a skin condition

content: : We need medical evidence that shows the condition was present within the first year after your last year of service in Vietnam.
Use Statement in Support of Claim (VA Form 21-4138) to answer the questions listed in this request under
To have us request your medical records, fill out General Release for Medical Provider Information (VA Form 21-4142).

blocks.content: Make sure your name and Social Security number are included on any document you upload or send.

blocks.content: Tell us the specific disease or disability that herbicide exposure (like Agent Orange) caused and provide medical evidence that shows:

items: What treatment you've received since discharge

content: Use Statement in Support of Claim (VA Form 21-4138) to answer the questions listed in this request under
To have us request your medical records, fill out General Release for Medical Provider Information (VA Form 21-4142).

ASB - tell us where, when, how exposed.shortDescription: To process your disability claim for asbestos exposure, we need a bit more information from you.
supportAliases: ASB - tell us where, when, how exposed

blocks.content: To process your disability claim for asbestos exposure, we need a bit more information from you.
Please answer the following questions:

items: Where were you exposed to asbestos? (Include your unit, rank, and location if you remember.)
Were any other service members with you? (Please share their names if you can.)
Were you exposed to anything else that may cause cancer (like cigarettes or chemicals) during or after your service?
What jobs did you have before and after your military service? How long did you work at each one?

content: Use Statement in Support of Claim (VA Form 21-4138) to answer the questions listed under
After completing and signing the form, you can upload or mail it.

ASB-medical evid of disease (biopsy) nee.shortDescription: We need medical documentation that supports your claim.

blocks.content: To review your claim for asbestos exposure, we need medical documentation that supports your claim.
This could include:
If the biopsy or other exam was done at a VA facility, upload a document that includes the location and the month and year of the exam. We’ll request your medical records for you.

content: If that exam was done by a non-VA provider, use VA Form 21-4142 to authorize VA to request your medical records.

ASB-tell us specific disability fm asbes.friendlyName: Disease or disability related to the asbestos exposure
supportAliases: ASB-tell us specific disability fm asbestos exposure

blocks.content: To process your disability claim for asbestos exposure, we need information about your asbestos-related disease or disability:

items: The specific disease or disability caused by asbestos exposure
Why you believe asbestos caused your disease or disability
Evidence of this connection, like a medical opinion from your doctor

content: Use Statement in Support of Claim (VA Form 21-4138) to answer the questions listed under
After completing and signing the form, you can upload or mail it.

Clarification of Claimed Issue.shortDescription: We need more information or a medical diagnosis for the condition in your benefits claim.

blocks.content: We need more information or a medical diagnosis for the condition in your benefits claim.
For example, if you listed a leg condition, we’d need to know if it affects your hip, knee, ankle, or foot—or if it’s related to nerves or your skin.

content: You can upload a document that gives more detail about your condition, or use VA Form 21-4138, Statement in Support of Claim.

blocks.content: Make sure your name and Social Security number are included on every page of any document you upload.

Employer (21-4192).shortDescription: We sent a letter to your last employer to ask about your job and why you left.

blocks.content: For your benefits claim, we sent a letter to your last employer to ask about your job and why you left. This information will help us understand if you left your job because of a service-connected disability.

Employment info needed.activityDescription: We need employment information from your most recent employer.

blocks.content: For your benefits claim, we need employment information from your most recent employer.

content: Give VA Form 21-4192 to your most recent employer and ask them to mail us your employment information.

Your health records may include:

blocks.content: For your benefits claim, we’ve requested your service treatment records from the Department of Defense.

NG1 - National Guard Records Request.shortDescription: We’ve asked your National Guard unit for your service treatment records.

blocks.content: For your benefits claim, we’ve asked your National Guard unit for your service treatment records. These records help us understand when your medical condition may have started during your military service.

Name of disability needed.shortDescription: We need to know what your disability is and how it’s connected to your military service.

blocks.content: To review your claim, we need to know what your disability is and how it’s connected to your military service.
Examples include:

items: Hearing loss from operating heavy equipment
Knee injury from a service-related accident
Post-traumatic stress disorder (PTSD) from exposure to traumatic events during service

blocks.content: If you have more than one disability, list each one in your claim.
Use VA Form 21-526EZ to provide the name of your disability and how it’s connected to your military service.

content: You can complete and sign this form online, or use a PDF version and upload or mail it.
Or, you can call the VA benefits hotline at
) to give us the name of your disability.

RADIATION - medical evid of disease need.shortDescription: To process your disability claim, we need medical information about your exposure to radiation.
supportAliases: RADIATION - medical evid of disease needed

blocks.content: To process your disability claim, we need information about your radiation-related disease or disability:

items: A medical report of your first diagnosis, or the date you were first diagnosed or treated
Names and addresses of all doctors who diagnosed or treated your condition
Complete medical records of all care you received for this condition during and after service
Reports about any tissue samples, slides, or pathology tests

blocks.content: We can help you get your medical records.

content.content: If a VA facility did your biopsy or other exam

content: , upload a document that lists the date and where the exam took place. We’ll request your medical records for you. You can use Statement in Support of Claim (VA Form 21-4138) for this.

content.content: If a non-VA provider did your exam

content: , you’ll need to authorize us to request your records. Fill out a General Release for Medical Provider Information (VA Form 21-4142).
Non-VA medical records are records from any private doctor, including a Veteran’s Choice doctor.

blocks.content: Make sure your name and Social Security number are included on any document you upload or send.

Radiation-tell us how you were exposed.shortDescription: To process your disability claim for radiation exposure, we need more information from you.
supportAliases: Radiation-tell us how you were exposed

blocks.content: To process your disability claim, we need more details about your radiation exposure during military service. We’ll use this information to determine your radiation exposure level. Use the "Radiation Risk Activity Information sheet" as a guide. This sheet is included with your claim letter.
Use the "Radiation Risk Activity Information sheet" that we included with your claim letter to gather the details you need.
After completing the sheet, you can upload or mail it.

Radiation-tell us specific disability fm.shortDescription: To process your disability claim, we need to know the disease or disability caused by the radiation exposure.
supportAliases: Radiation-tell us specific disability fm exposure

blocks.content: Tell us which disease or disability the radiation exposure caused so we can continue processing your claim.

content: Use Statement in Support of Claim (VA Form 21-4138) to answer the questions listed under

Request Service Treatment Records from V.shortDescription: We need certified copies of your service treatment records if you have them.
supportAliases: Request Service Treatment Records from Veteran

blocks.content: For your benefits claim, we need certified copies of your service treatment records if you have them. Uploading your copies may speed up the claim review process.

In case you don’t have them, we also requested your service treatment records from the Department of Defense, reserve unit, or national guard unit. You don’t need to request your service treatment records yourself.
Once we have your service treatment records we can schedule an exam.
Upload certified copies of your service treatment records, if you have them. This can help speed up the claim review process.
If you don’t have certified copies of your service treatment records, no action is needed. We are requesting them for you.

RV1 - Reserve Records Request.activityDescription: We’ve requested your reserve records on your behalf. No action is needed.
RV1 - Reserve Records Request.shortDescription: We’ve requested your service records or treatment records from your reserve unit.

blocks.content: For your benefits claim, we’ve requested your service records or treatment records from your reserve unit.
Your records may be used to verify:

SSA medical evidence requested.friendlyName: Medical records from the Social Security Administration
SSA medical evidence requested.shortDescription: We’ve asked the Social Security Administration (SSA) for your medical records.

blocks.content: For your benefits claim, we’ve asked the Social Security Administration (SSA) for your medical records. This helps us understand your condition and how it might relate to your claim.
It may take several months to receive a response from the SSA.

Submit buddy statement(s).shortDescription: We need statements from people who know about your condition.

blocks.content: For your disability benefits claim, we need statements from people who know about your condition. These are often called "buddy statements."
These people should:

items: Describe what they saw or know about your condition
Include when and where they observed it, and what was happening at the time
Say clearly if they witnessed the event that caused your condition
If they served with you, include their name, rank, and unit at the time

blocks.content: Each person must include the following sentence at the end of their statement:
"I hereby certify that this information is true and correct to the best of my knowledge and belief."
Use VA Form 21-10210 to submit a Lay/Witness Statement (also known as "buddy statements") from someone who knows about your condition and can support your claim.

content: You can submit the completed form online, or you can upload or mail it.

Unemployability - 21-8940 needed and 419.shortDescription: We need more information about how your service-connected disabilities prevent you from working.

blocks.content: For your benefits claim, we need more information about how your service-connected disabilities prevent you from working.
We also need information from your most recent employer to tell us what your role was and the reasons why your employment ended.

content: Use
to let us know what service-connected disabilities prevent you from working. You can complete and sign this form online, or use a PDF version and upload or mail it.
Give
to your most recent employer and ask them to mail us your employment information.
